items.title: 'Finding hidden gems' in 100 years of soil stored at national archive

children.content: Far from brown dirt, the Australian National Soil Archive houses 100,000 soil samples — some a century old — that represent a slice through time of the ground beneath our feet.

gallery.altText: Three old jars of soil with faded labels.

thumbnail.altText: Three old jars of soil with faded labels.

items.title: Rattenbury denies quitting over coalition talks, but they can't have helped

children.content: In announcing his retirement from ACT politics, Greens leader Shane Rattenbury says he is looking forward to finding out what his next career move could be.

image.altText: A man with short hair wearing a blazer stands outdoors looking serious.

items.title: Educators plead not guilty to assaulting students in Queanbeyan school

children.content: Four educators accused of assaulting children, aged 9 and 10, at a Queanbeyan school were not present in court when their lawyers indicated they would fight the charges.

image.altText: A building with a sign 'Queanbeyan Courthouse'

items.title: 'All you want as a parent': The disability program leading to home ownership

children.content: Not-for-profit Project Independence uses residents' disability support pension to share in a property, giving them legal ownership of their home and allowing them to build equity over time.

gallery.altText: A woman with long dark hair stands smiling outdoors, with a man and woman with grey hair on either side of her.

thumbnail.altText: A woman with long dark hair stands smiling outdoors, with a man and woman with grey hair on either side of her.

items.title: Four metres of WWII 'short snorter' banknotes signify mateship

children.content: It is a crime to deface banknotes, but these rare wartime notes covered in scribbles cause excitement whenever they appear.

image.altText: man in checkered shirt sitting at table with rows of coloured banknotes

items.title: Mystery of WWI digger puzzles NSW community

children.content: The names etched into the Araluen War Memorial are read aloud every Anzac Day, but one digger from World War I remains a mystery.

image.altText: Two women standing in front of a war memorial

items.title: 'Feel it when I breathe': Canberra's first dedicated vaping support program opens

children.content: The percentage of young people who have tried a vape has almost tripled since 2018, but Canberra's first dedicated vaping support program says young people are taking it upon themselves to overcome addiction.

gallery.altText: A man with short dark hair sits on an outdoor bench vaping.

thumbnail.altText: A man with short dark hair sits on an outdoor bench vaping.

items.title: New scooters and e-bikes have arrived in Canberra. Here's what that means

children.content: With Neuron's orange scooters having left Canberra, Lime has introduced a fleet of green scooters and e-bikes across the capital in partnership with the ACT government.

image.altText: A person rides a green Lime bike near a fountain in an urban space.

items.title: Arrest warrant issued in legal battle for $27k in unpaid long service leave

children.content: It's now been over three years since two women left their jobs at a small business. Two courts later, they want their combined $27,000 in long service leave paid out to them, and say they won't give up until they get it.

image.altText: two women sitting at a dining table looking through paperwork

items.title: China suddenly grants new beef licences to Australia

children.content: China has granted beef export approval to several new Australian facilities, just months after introducing a range of trade measures to protect its domestic cattle industry.

gallery.altText: Chef cooking beef with China and Australian flags in the background

thumbnail.altText: Chef cooking beef with China and Australian flags in the background

items.title: Government pours $30m into counter-drone technology in wake of Iran war

children.content: The Australian government signs two contracts to develop counter-drone technology, including a laser that can track an object the size of a 10-cent coin travelling at more than 100 km/h.

image.altText: A small grey drone sits on a table with Sypaq banner in the background.

items.title: Sacked civil servant describes pressure to approve Mandelson as UK ambassador to US

children.content: The former head of the Foreign Office said there was "a very, very strong expectation" that Peter Mandelson "needed to be in post and in America as quickly as possible".

image.altText: A man in a black scarf walks sternly.

items.title: Boyd says other AFL players are struggling 'under the surface' after Hollands episode

children.content: Tom Boyd, former AFL footballer turned mental health advocate, says the high-pressure environment of professional football means many players are struggling with mental health issues "under the surface".

gallery.altText: Tom Boyd speaks during a tribute to Danny Frawley before an AFL game.

thumbnail.altText: Tom Boyd speaks during a tribute to Danny Frawley before an AFL game.

items.title: 'Outrageous mess': About 2,000 AI road safety camera fines withdrawn in WA

children.content: About 2,000 fines valued at $1.1 million issued as a result of AI-assisted road safety cameras in WA have been withdrawn, six months after the devices were introduced.

image.altText: A screenshot of a fine issues for a seatbelt issue

items.title: More uncounted votes discovered in SA a month after election

children.content: More uncounted votes have been discovered in two electorates a month on from the South Australian state election, and while the state's Electoral Commission says the ballots will not harm the "integrity" of the count, they have not said how many have been found.

image.altText: A ballot box during an election.

items.title: Diesel and fertiliser are shooting up in price

children.content: There are two things crucial to modern farming - synthetic fertiliser and diesel.
So what happens when the cost of both increases massively, but the crops they're used to grow remains the same price?
That's precisely the dilemma facing Australian farmers as they prepare to plant their winter crops for this year.
While some have chosen to opt out of the season entirely, others say they are pushing ahead and facing the uncertainty.

image.altText: A person with a beard is holding a jar of small white beads and smiling slightly. The setting is a bright room.

gallery.altText: A person with a beard is holding a jar of small white beads and smiling slightly. The setting is a bright room.

thumbnail.altText: A person with a beard is holding a jar of small white beads and smiling slightly. The setting is a bright room.

items.title: Producer on Rebel Wilson film branded 'Indian Ghislaine Maxwell', court hears

children.content: Australian actor Rebel Wilson asked her crisis communications firm to publish a website that accused a producer on her own film of being an "Indian Ghislaine Maxwell", a Sydney court hears.

image.altText: Rebel Wilson arrives at court

items.title: Earthquake hits off coast of Timor

children.content: The epicentre was at a depth of 31 kilometres but there was no tsunami risk from the magnitude-6 quake, according to Indonesia's geophysics agency.

image.altText: Satellite map showing where an earthquake has hit near Timor-Leste

items.title: Six ways your smartwatch is lying to you, according to science

children.content: Wearable fitness technologies like smartwatches are worn by millions of people daily. But they're not as accurate as you might think.

gallery.altText: A woman in a red T-shirt looks down at a watch next to swimming pool

thumbnail.altText: A woman in a red T-shirt looks down at a watch next to swimming pool

items.title: I quit TV for a month, but the trick was what I did with myself instead

children.content: It was partly an experiment in self-control and habit-breaking, but also a response to the unhappy realisation that watching shows had become my crutch to zone out.

image.altText: A woman sits in her front garden, which she replanted with native plants to attract local butterflies and insects.

avatar.alt: A woman sits in her front garden, which she replanted with native plants to attract local butterflies and insects.

items.title: Living in an apartment with your dog? Here are four things to consider

children.content: Providing opportunities for your dog to explore, giving them a safe and comfortable space to retreat to — as well as a good routine — are key to keeping your dog happy.

image.altText: small brown dog laying in blue furry bed

items.title: Researchers develop surfboard fins with shark-deterrent technology

children.content: A multi-million-dollar project run out of the University of Wollongong is working on fitting shark-deterrent technology into surfboard fins following recent shark attacks.

gallery.altText: Man sanding surfboard in lab, wearing facemask and protective glasses

thumbnail.altText: Man sanding surfboard in lab, wearing facemask and protective glasses

items.title: Woolworths's 'Prices Dropped' promotion broke guidelines, court hears

children.content: The consumer watchdog has taken Woolworths to court, claiming shoppers weren't receiving genuine discounts on hundreds of items.

image.altText: Close up of the green Woolworths W logo on the outside of a building.

items.title: Man jailed for mimicking shooting on bridge used in Bondi attack

children.content: A man who yelled antisemitic profanities while pretending to shoot at people from a pedestrian bridge used by the Bondi Beach terror attack gunmen has been sentenced.

image.altText: Bondi Beach bridge three weeks after terrorist attack.

items.title: Bitter with Australia for deporting him, he hatched a plan to get even

children.content: Eneasi Taumoefolau's 2024 arrest on drugs charges ignited fears the notoriously violent outlaw Comancheros gang had infiltrated "the Friendly Islands".

image.altText: A man sits for an interview.

items.title: Legal action considered after Dezi Freeman property entry

children.content: Property owner considers legal action after people enter the Upper Murray property where Dezi Freeman was shot.

image.altText: Dezi Freeman pictured outside a court in Wangaratta.

items.title: Last chance for answers as police admit error in search for Nannup Four

children.content: The family of a mother and daughter who went missing, along with two others, in WA almost 20 years ago are pinning their hopes on a last-ditch effort to uncover the truth of what became of them.

gallery.altText: Two men, a woman and a child holding their hands in a prayer pose.

thumbnail.altText: Two men, a woman and a child holding their hands in a prayer pose.

items.title: Charges over environmental impacts from cement ship crash with tugboats

children.content: The owner of a bulk cement carrier has been charged with environmental offences following a 2022 crash that sank two tugboats in north-west Tasmania.

image.altText: A tugboat being lifted by a crane.

items.title: Residents urged to evacuate New Zealand's capital after floods

children.content: People living in low-lying areas of New Zealand's capital city, Wellington, are urged to evacuate as heavy rain drenches the city.

image.altText: car on a fence.

items.title: NRL overhauls training rules in landmark player safety move

children.content: The move to restrict training loads is aimed at mitigating injuries, including player head injuries, which continue to be a major issue for all contact sports.

gallery.altText: a rugby league coach at training

thumbnail.altText: a rugby league coach at training

items.title: 'Pretty invisible': The rise of ADHD medication being prescribed to young women

children.content: Within the sharp increase of ADHD rates in Australia lies a hidden story — more women are being prescribed medication than men. Understanding why is more complicated.

image.altText: A graphic image with silhouettes of men and women's side profiles overlaid with medication and two upwards arrows.

items.title: Controversial rapper's bodyguard allegedly assaulted in Sydney abduction

children.content: Five people have been arrested after police rescued a controversial rapper's bodyguard who was allegedly kidnapped from his Guildford home.

image.altText: Forensic detectives remove evidence from an abduction scene.

items.title: El Salvador holds mass trial of alleged gang members for 29,000 murders

children.content: The attorney-general says 486 suspected MS-13 members have faced trial for 47,000 crimes committed between 2012 and 2022.

gallery.altText: several rows of inmates dressed in white sit down inside a prison hall

thumbnail.altText: several rows of inmates dressed in white sit down inside a prison hall

items.title: Paramedics unable to help bleeding mother for almost an hour, inquest hears

children.content: A paramedic tells an inquest she decided to wait for police back-up before attending to a woman who was actively harming herself in a Northern Territory domestic violence shelter.

image.altText: The exterior of the Darwin Local Court.

items.title: Festival ticketholders waiting six years for refund after cancelled shows

children.content: Ticketholders are still waiting for refunds and answers from The Drop music festival.

image.altText: Audience and lit up festival stage.
